REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ROBERT POYNTER, NILI POYNTER NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXPUNGE NOTICE OF PENDENCY OF ACTION, AND FOR ATTORNEYS FEES IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 26, 2020 FOR PARTIES TO SUBMIT SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING. BY NO LATER THAN AUGUST 12, 2020 PLAINTIFF TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF ADDRESSING THE FOLLOWING ISSUES: (1) CAN A SECOND LIS PENDENS BE RECORDED WHILE THERE IS AN ALLEGEDLY VALID LIS PENDENS ALREADY ON THE RECORD. RESPONSE FORMAT SHALL BE "YES" OR "NO" WITH CITATION TO AUTHORITY FOLLOWED; (2) DOES THE RECORDATION OF THE SECOND LIS PENDENS WITHDRAW THE RECORDATION OF THE FIRST LIS PENDENS. RESPONSE FORMAT SHALL BE "YES" OR "NO" WITH CITATION TO AUTHORITY FOLLOWED; (3) LIST EVIDENCE SUPPORTING EACH ELEMENT OF PARTITION CAUSE OF ACTION SUBMITTED WITH THE OPPOSITION BY REFERENCE THE DOCUMENT AND PAGE/PARAGRAPH NUMBER. RESPONSE SHOULD PREFERABLY BE IN A TABLE FORMAT. COLUMN ONE TO STATE THE ELEMENT; COLUMN TWO TO LIST THE EVIDENCE, E.G. DECLARATION OF JOHN SMITH, PARAGRAPH 3 AND/OR EXHIBIT 2, PAGE 4, PARAGRAPH 3. DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A RESPONSE TO THE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF (IN THE SAME FORMAT) NO LATER THAN AUGUST 19, 2020. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ROBERT POYNTER AND NILI POYNTER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF A REFEREE TO EXAMINE ACCOUNT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF REFEREE IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: 1) PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING A REFEREE AND SUBMIT A JOINT PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT NO LATER THAN JANUARY 13, 2022. THE ORDER SHALL ALSO SPECIFY THE DEADLINE FOR REFEREE'S REPORT UNDER CCP 643 AND FOR ANY MOTIONS CHALLENGING SUCH REPORT; OR 2) IF PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A REFEREE, EACH PARTY (PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER IF SUBMISSIONS WILL BE PER SIDE OR PER PARTY) SHALL SUBMIT A LIST OF 3 PROPOSED REFEREES, READY AND WILLING TO SERVE NO LATER THAN JANUARY 13, 2022. EACH PARTY MAY OBJECT TO ONE PROPOSED REFEREE NO LATER THAN JANUARY 17, 2022. DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AN ORDER APPOINTING A REFEREE LEAVING THE NAME OF THE REFEREE BLANK. THE ORDER SHALL ALSO SPECIFY THE DEADLINE FOR REFEREE'S REPORT UNDER CCP 643 AND FOR ANY MOTIONS CHALLENGING SUCH REPORT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar May-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. STEPHEN B. WELCH, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 925-357-3456) for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Doran Construction & Design, Inc. and Cross-Defendant Brad Doran;
Atty. ANTHONY D. GILES, Esq. of Law Office of Anthony Giles (Tel: 415-839-2099) for Defendants/Cross-Complainants Robert Poynter and Nili Poynter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ROBERT POYNTER AND NILI POYNTER's Motion To Compel Response To Interrogatory, And For Monetary Sanctions.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is denied. As drafted, Special Interrogatory No. 419 asks the responding party (DCD) to provide information for every line item in 59 invoices, which is overbroad on its face and would necessitate the preparation or the making of a compilation, abstract, audit, or summary of entries in files and records of DCD. DCD's answer complies with CCP 2030.230, in that it refers to the statute and specifies the writings from which the answer may be derived or ascertained-that is, the 59 bundles of documents that DCD produced, with each bundle containing an invoice and all numbered documents that support the charges on that invoice. DCD's specification provides sufficient detail to permit Poynter to locate and to identify, as readily as DCD can, the documents from which the answer may be ascertained on an invoice-by-invoice basis. To the extent there may be confusion about particular invoices or line items, counsel could have engaged in further meet and confer to resolve such confusion prior to filing the instant motion and/or narrowed the requests.

Counsel for Doran Construction is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com (cc'ing all parties).

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT DORAN CONSTRUCTION & DESIGN INC., BRAD DORAN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF ROBERT POYNTER AND NILI POYNTER.

Cross-Defendants Doran Construction & Design, Inc. and Brad Doran's demurrer to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and the eighth cause of action for fraud in the cross-complaint filed by Robert and Nili Poynter is overruled as to all three causes of action. The cross-complaint does not show that the negligent misrepresentation and fraud claims are necessarily barred by the statute of limitations. Assuming the limitations period began to run in April 2016, the cross-complaint was filed within three years of the filing of this lawsuit. (ZF Micro Devices, Inc. v. TAT Capital Partners, Ltd. (2016) 5 Cal.App.5th 69, 84 [Cross-complaint "relates back" to the date the plaintiff's action was filed, and hence avoids the statute of limitations].) The fraud and negligent misrepresentation claims are alleged with sufficient specificity and are based on alleged ultimate facts supporting each of the required elements. The alleged estimate of the construction budget is an actionable affirmation of fact. (Cicone v. URS Corp. (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 194, 202 ["[A] statement by an architect that a building will not cost more than a certain amount may be regarded as an affirmation of fact."]]. "A statement couched as an opinion, by one having special knowledge of the subject, may be treated as an actionable misstatement of fact." (Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. The Americana at Brand, LLC (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1230, 1241.) Paragraph 7 of the cross-complaint alleges that the Poynters had no expertise in establishing the costs, time, design, or construction and that they relied on Mr. Doran to provide accurate numbers. A huge disparity between the estimates and the ultimate costs supports an inference of misrepresentation. (Id. at 1242, fn. 7.) Paragraph 56 of the cross-complaint alleges that Mr. Doran gave the Poynters an artificially low number for the budget and had no intention of sticking to it. Paragraph 58 of the cross-complaint alleges that Mr. Doran intentionally concealed his intentions, continued to bill the Poynters, lied to the Poynters, and used the Poynters as a bank to fund the project. The Poynters also adequately state a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)
